augur.name: Augur
description: ## Augur
‹SOURCE Arcane Anthology/25›

Tales of Merivesta Olinchi are surprisingly common among the Bekyar of the Mwangi Expanse, who claim she spent some time studying their traditions and history. Most historians find it more likely that a Bekyar skald spent time in Nex, was exposed to the idea of divination as a tool of war from a copy of ‹eq-spellbook/Cold Irony›, and fabricated tales of how that knowledge came to be in his hands.

::ab[Monster Insight (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost ability="The augur applies his Charisma modifier in addition to his Intelligence modifier when attempting Knowledge checks to identify the abilities and weaknesses of creatures." special="Monster insight counts as the *monster lore* class feature for prerequisites." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Signs and Portents (Su)]{icon=magic l2="An augur can read signs and portents to attempt to ascertain how events in the near future will play out. This allows the augur to cast ‹spell/augury› as a spell-like ability once a day." l7="He can instead use *signs and portents* to cast ‹spell/divination› as a spell-like ability." imp12="He can instead cast ‹spell/commune› as a spell-like ability." imp17="He can use *signs and portents* twice per day." replace="All types of versatile performance"}

::ab[Predictive Strike (Su)]{l=5 icon=melee flavor="The augur accurately divines the possible movements of his enemies in combat." free="Once per day, he can declare one of his melee attacks to be a *predictive strike.* Against the augur's attack, the target is ‹rule/flat-footed› and gains no benefits from ‹rule/concealment› or ‹rule/cover›. Attacks from other creatures are treated normally. The target still benefits from total concealment and total cover normally." imp11="The augur can use this ability on a total of two attacks per day." imp17="He can use this on three attacks per day." replace="Spell kenning"}

bacchanal.name: Bacchanal
description: ## Bacchanal
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/84›

Bacchanals are skalds who use ecstatic dancing and ribald songs to influence the primal instincts of listeners. To these skalds, the passionate frenzy of a celebration or feast shares many features with the violence of combat, and several of their talents blur the line between festival and battle in dramatic ways.

::ab[Fermented Fruit (Sp)]{icon=learn-magic-def ability="A bacchanal adds ‹spell/goodberry› to his list of 1st-level skald spells known. In addition, he can cast it as a spell-like ability once per day. When he casts *goodberry* as a spell or spell-like ability, the targeted berries ferment; this causes each berry to act as a serving of an alcoholic beverage. A creature that eats a fermented berry does not gain a meal's nourishment, but it is cured of 1 point of damage and becomes slightly foolhardy as if tipsy, gaining a +1 bonus on saves against fear effects for 1 minute after eating the berry." replace="Scribe Scroll"}

::ab[Drunken Dancer (Su)]{l=2 icon=power-magic-def-boost provokes=No move="While maintaining a *raging song,* a bacchanal can consume a berry affected by *goodberry,* a potion, or a serving of alcohol. A berry affected by *goodberry* or a potion has its normal effect, while an alcoholic drink or a berry affected by *goodberry* via *fermented fruit* (see above) allows the skald to maintain his *raging song* that round without expending a round of *raging song* for the day (instead of the normal effects of the alcohol or fermented berry)." special="For each alcoholic drink or fermented berry consumed while raging, the skald is ‹misc/nauseated› for 1 round after his *raging song* ends. The *internal fortitude* rage power prevents this nauseated condition from occurring." imp7="When the bacchanal consumes an alcoholic drink or fermented berry while maintaining his rage song, he can gain the benefits of ‹spell/cure light wounds› or ‹spell/remove fear› (for the duration of the *raging song*) at his caster level by spending 2 rounds of *raging song.*" imp12="The bacchanal can gain the benefits of ‹spell/heroism› (for the duration of the *raging song*) in this manner." imp17="He can gain the benefits of ‹spell/persistent vigor› (for the duration of the *raging song*) in this manner." replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head info="A bacchanal gains access to the following *raging songs.*" icon=power}

::ab[Song of Urging (Su)]{next l=3 icon=power flavor="The bacchanal's *raging song* can wordlessly influence creatures of the wild." ability="This *raging song* functions just like a ‹skill/Diplomacy› check to make a simple request or an Intimidate check but can influence only animals. The bacchanal rolls 1d20 and adds his skald level and his Charisma modifier to determine the *urging* check's result. Bonuses and penalties that apply to wild empathy checks apply to these *urging* checks. The typical domestic animal has a starting attitude of indifferent, while wild animals are usually unfriendly. The song can influence plants or magical beasts with Intelligence scores of 1 or 2 as well as drunken creatures of any Intelligence (even without sharing a language), but against these non-animals, the bacchanal takes a -4 penalty on this check. The bacchanal must continue to perform the song for the entire time required for the check; otherwise, its effect is wasted, but only 1 round of *raging song* is expended regardless of how long the check takes." replace="Song of marching"}

::ab[Maddening Dance (Su)]{next l=10 icon=lower order=an ability="The bacchanal's *raging song* can compel a creature within 30 feet to perform an ecstatic dance. Unless it succeeds at its Will save, the victim becomes ‹misc/confused›, takes a -2 penalty to its AC and on Reflex saving throws, and loses the benefit of any shield it holds. The effect ends when the target can no longer hear the bacchanal from within 30 feet." info="As an @HLimmediate_action, an ally of the bacchanal within 30 feet can dance along as a @HLfull-round_action (taking all penalties except the confused condition for that round) to allow the bacchanal to target an additional creature." special="A creature that succeeds at the Will saving throw against this *raging song* is immune to this effect for 24 hours. This is a compulsion, emotion, enchantment, and mind-affecting effect." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::list[The following rage powers complement the bacchanal archetype]{hl link=ragepower all="animal fury~boasting taunt~fearless rage~fierce fortitude~increased damage reduction~internal fortitude~liquid courage~night vision~roaring drunk~staggering drunk"}

battle_scion.name: Battle Scion
description: ## Battle Scion
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/48›

The battle scion possesses a unique mixture of courtly grace alongside martial and magical prowess. She is a warrior-poet, as skilled with her words as she is with blades. Battle scions sometimes rule as queens over courts of brave knights who scour the world championing bold quests.

::ab[Courtly Presence (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost-down flavor="A battle scion's bearing radiates command and presence, allowing her to tower over her opponents in social situations." ability="The battle scion adds 1/2 her character level to ‹skill/Intimidate› checks, and she starts any ‹rule/verbal duel<s› with an extra edge that she can use for the presence tactic so long as she is not at a significant or extreme disadvantage in the duel. However, her bardic knowledge ability applies to only Knowledge (geography), Knowledge (history), Knowledge (local), and Knowledge (nobility)." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Battle Prowess (Ex)]{icon=power ability="Whenever a battle scion would gain a rage power from the skald's *rage power* ability (at 3rd level and every 3 skald levels thereafter), she can instead choose to gain a ‹main/combat feat› or ‹main/teamwork feat› for which she meets the prerequisites. Like rage powers she selects in this way, the battle scion can grant this feat to allies under the effect of *inspired rage,* as long as those allies also meet the prerequisites, but she must expend 2 rounds of *raging song* for each round in which she grants a combat feat or teamwork feat." special="If the battle scion gains multiple feats from *battle prowess,* allies can use these feats to meet the prerequisites for other feats while they're affected by *battle prowess* (for example, a battle scion with ‹feat/Power Attack› and ‹feat/Cleave› from *battle prowess* could grant both, allowing allies with a +1 base attack bonus and a Strength score of 13 to gain both, since they can use Power Attack to qualify for Cleave)." replace="Rage power"}
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head icon=power info="A battle scion gains the following *raging song.*"}

::ab[Song of Questing (Su)]{next l=10 icon=magic-warning order=an ability="The battle scion can spend 4 rounds of *raging song* to draw upon a target's honor, ambition, and sense of self in order to bind the target to an agreed-upon task, often a noble quest. This works like ‹spell/geas/quest›, except it applies only if the target is truly willing (not magically compelled or tricked), and the target automatically understands the full terms of the agreement before deciding whether he is willing." info="The battle scion can offer a reward for the completion of the noble quest, but if she does so and fails to live up to her agreement, she becomes an ex-battle scion and loses all benefits gained from the archetype (she does not gain the abilities they replaced, either)." imp14="She can offer the same quest to up to one willing creature per skald level. If she does so, the effect ends for all creatures as soon as anyone completes the quest, though only the creature who completes it earns any offered reward." replace="Dirge of doom~song of the fallen"}

::ab[Once and Future Scion (Su)]{l=20 icon=magic-def flavor="A battle scion becomes extremely difficult to kill." passive="Whenever she is slain, as long as her body isn't destroyed, she enters a deathlike sleep for 3 days, after which she returns to life as per ‹spell/raise dead›, except that it can restore her even if she was slain by a death effect. Spells that would restore her to life function as if she were dead during this time, allowing her to recover sooner." replace="Master skald"}

bekyar_demon_dancer.name: Bekyar Demon Dancer
description: ## Bekyar Demon Dancer
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/18›
While the twisted power of the demon-worshiping Bekyar's skalds is great, it comes at a terrible price.
::cskill{gain="perf|dance"}

::ab[Versatile Performance (Ex)]{icon=power info="A Bekyar demon dancer can choose Perform (dance) for the *versatile performance* class feature. Its associated skills are Acrobatics and Fly." alter="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Fiendish Maw (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="Whenever a Bekyar demon dancer *inspires rage,* all who accept his rage gain a bite attack dealing 1d6 points of damage (for Medium allies; 1d4 points of damage if Small). If used as part of a full attack action, the bite attack is made at the creature's full base attack bonus - 5." imp7="The bite damage increases to 1d8 (1d6 if Small)." imp12="The bite becomes 2d6 (1d8 if Small)." imp17="The bite increases to 3d6 (2d6 if Small)." special="The increases to bite damage effectively treat the affected creatures as though their sizes had increased by one size category, and so don't stack with any similar damage increases (such as the increase from the ‹spell/strong jaw› spell) other than those due to an actual increase in size." replace="Rage power gained at 3rd level"}

::ab[Abyssal Wrath (Su)]{icon=power l6="The Bekyar demon dancer gains the ‹ragepower/fiend totem› rage power, even if he doesn't qualify for it." l9="He gains the ‹ragepower/greater fiend totem› rage power, ignoring the prerequisites." alter="Rage powers gained at 6th and 9th levels"}

::ab[Demonic Conquest (Su)]{l=7 icon=magic flavor="The Bekyar demon dancer's dances warp the minds of those who accept them even further." ability="Whenever anyone affected by his *inspired rage* makes an attack against a creature, the urge to dominate this foe rises within the affected ally. The ally can attempt to resist this urge with a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the skald's class level + his Charisma modifier). If she fails the save or chooses not to attempt one, the Strength and Constitution bonuses of the demon dancer's *inspire rage* increase by 2, and she must continue to attack the same target each round." special="Once the target is defeated, she must burn or cut the symbol of a demon the Bekyar demon dancer worships into the target's flesh, which is a @HLfull-round_action. The need to conquer and brand foes ends after this action or after the demon dancer stops performing, whichever comes first."}

belkzen_war_drummer.name: Belkzen War Drummer
description: ## Belkzen War Drummer
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/19›

These fierce drummers are equally adept at tapping out a driving rhythm and rapping enemies upside the head with the same massive clubs they use to beat the crude hidecovered drums they carry into battle.
::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=down info="A war drummer is proficient with all simple weapons and the ‹eq-weapon/greatclub›."}
::ab[Fearsome Mien (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A war drummer adds 1/2 his class level (minimum 1) to all Intimidate and Bluff skill checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Deadly Rhythm (Ex)]{icon=power-boost l1="During his *raging song,* a Belkzen war drummer can draw ‹eq-weapon/club«s› and greatclubs as a @HLfree_action as if he had the ‹feat/Quick Draw› feat." l3="The war drummer gains a +1 bonus on damage rolls for attacks made with clubs or greatclubs." l6="The war drummer gains ‹feat/Improved Critical› with the club or the greatclub as a bonus feat." replace="Scribe Scroll" increment="This bonus~3~4"}
::ab[Weapon Master (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A war drummer gains ‹feat/Craft Magic Arms and Armor› as a bonus feat." replace="Lore master"}
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head info="A war drummer gains access to the following *raging song.*" icon=power}

::ab[Siege Drums (Su)]{next l=7 icon=magic ability="The Belkzen war drummer can shatter massive walls, doors, and other inanimate obstacles using his war drums. This ability works as the ‹spell/shatter› spell, except the drummer can increase the amount of damage and size of the object damaged by playing for longer periods. The siege drummer can affect objects weighing up to 10 pounds per level, plus an additional 5 pounds per level for each additional round he plays. The damage dealt increases by 2d6 per round (to a maximum of 20d6 after 10 rounds). The damage is dealt only after the drummer ceases playing." replace="7th-level versatile performance gained benefit"}

boaster.name: Boaster
description: ## Boaster
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/84›

Boasters use incredible tales and claims to challenge themselves and their allies to accomplish remarkable feats. With a properly delivered boast, a boaster can inspire listeners to attempt, and more often than not, succeed at stunts or tasks they may not have realized they were capable of doing.
::ab[Endurance]{icon=power ability="The boaster gains ‹feat/Endurance› as a bonus feat." replace="Scribe Scroll"}
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head info="A boaster gains access to the following *raging songs.*" icon=power}

::ab[Song of Endurance (Su)]{next l=3 icon=boost ability="The boaster's *raging song* can inspire his allies to great feats of endurance. This *raging song* functions as *song of marching* except affected allies also gain the benefits of the Endurance feat in addition to the benefits of any of the following feats the boaster has (even if they don't meet the prerequisites): ‹feat/Deathless Initiate›, ‹feat/Deathless Master›, ‹feat/Deathless Zealot›, ‹feat/Diehard›, ‹feat/Fast Healer›, ‹feat/Heroic Defiance›, and ‹feat/Heroic Recovery›." replace="Uncanny dodge" alter="Song of marching"}

::ab[Song of Surmounting (Su)]{next l=7 icon=power ability="The boaster's *raging song* can inspire allies to move quickly and easily. By spending 1 round of *raging song,* the boaster can affect all allies within 90 feet for 1 hour. Affected allies gain a benefit according to the surrounding terrain (the benefits don't stack): a climb speed equal to half their base speed (forest, jungle, mountain, underground), a swim speed equal to their base speed (swamp, water), or a +10 foot enhancement bonus to their base speed (cold, desert, hill, plains, or urban). The skald must continue to perform the song for the remainder of the hour; otherwise, its effects end, but only 1 round of *raging song* is expended for that hour." imp13="The speed or bonus granted by this ability doubles." imp19="This ability grants triple the listed speed or bonus." replace="Lore master"}
::ab[Frightful Boast (Su)]{next l=14 icon=lower ability="The boaster's *raging song* can frighten foes, as per the ‹performance/frightening tune› bardic performance." replace="Song of the fallen"}
::ab[Rage Power (Ex)]{icon=down info="The boaster does not gain this ability until 4th level." alter="Rage powers"}

::ab[Greater Endurance]{l=6 icon=power ability="The boaster gains a bonus feat that he qualifies for. The feat must have Endurance as a prerequisite (such as Diehard or Fast Healer)." replace="Rage power gained at 6th level"}

::list[The following rage powers complement the boaster archetype]{hl link=ragepower all="boasting taunt~come and get me~fearless rage~fierce fortitude~intimidating glare~raging climber~raging flier~raging leaper~raging swimmer~sprint~strength surge~swift foot"}

bold_schemer.name: Bold Schemer
description: ## Bold Schemer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/48›

The bold schemer combines wrath, wits, and daring to win battles and wars in unconventional ways, often through infiltration and deception. If raw force of arms is insufficient, she improvises ways to pull victory from the jaws of defeat.
::cskill{gain=dd~soh~stl lose=ka~kd~kp}
::ab[Bardic knowledge]{icon=down info="A bold schemer's *bardic knowledge* does not apply to Knowledge (arcana), Knowledge (dungeoneering), or Knowledge (planes)." alter="Bardic knowledge"}

:::ab{l=4 icon=boost title="Skald of Twists and Turns (Su)" action=Ability flavor="A bold schemer becomes increasingly adept at infiltration and devising cunning strategies to overcome enemy fortifications." increment="The insight bonus~4~4~4~2" replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge~damage reduction"}

A bold schemer can spend 8 hours observing a physical location (such as a fortress, a walled city, or perhaps even a section of an underground dungeon or cavern complex), contemplating methods to infiltrate the location and engage its defenders in battle in ways that subvert their defensive advantages.

At the end of the 8 hours, the bold schemer gains a +2 insight bonus on her Bluff, Disable Device, Disguise, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth checks while in that same physical location. The bonus lasts until the bold schemer observes a new location. While observing a location, the bold schemer must fully focus her attention; any distractions, such as engaging in combat, immediately ends the observation and the bold schemer must start a new period of observing a physical location to receive the bonus.

::ab[Bold Strategy (Su)]{l=10 icon=boost flavor="A bold schemer can tailor her plans and observations so they are able to assist her allies as well." passive="As long as they can see or hear the bold schemer while inside the observed location, all of her allies gain half the insight bonus she gains from the *skald of twists and turns* ability." replace="Dirge of doom"}

court_poet.name: Court Poet
description: ## Court Poet
‹SOURCE Heroes of the High Court/6›

Many courts are places of artistic refinement, attracting those performers who wish to revel in an aristocratic art scene. Such artists may aim to become a darling of the court, focusing on the aesthetic requirements of a particular tradition as well as learning details about that court's history and culture. Court poets elevate the skald's love of history and poetry to an aristocratic ideal, captivating courts with complicated poetic traditions and inspiring others with their craft. Some court poets go on to create their own works, weaving their magic and force of personality into their unique performances.

::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head icon=power info="A court poet gains the following *raging songs,* allowing her to use oratory, music, and other performances to elevate the aesthetic sensibilities of her allies."}

::ab[Insightful Contemplation (Su)]{next l=1 icon=boost-down ability="Affected allies gain a +2 morale bonus to Intelligence and Charisma and a +1 morale bonus on Will saving throws, but they also take a -1 penalty to AC. While under the effects of *insightful contemplation,* allies other than the court poet can't use any Strength-based skills or make any physical effort that requires a Constitution check." incrementMulti="The song's bonus on Will saves increases to +~.~0/4;2/1~~The song's bonuses to Intelligence and Charisma become +~.~0/8;4/2" special="Unlike the barbarian's rage ability, those affected are not fatigued after the song ends." replace="Inspired rage"}

::ab[Song of Inspiration (Su)]{next l=6 icon=boost flavor="A court poet can use *raging song* to inspire her allies to greater mental clarity." ability="Once each round while the court poet uses this performance, allies within 60 feet who can hear her can add 1/2 the court poet's skald level to a single Wisdom check or Wisdom-based skill check." replace="Song of strength"}

::ab[Handling the Crowd (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def-boost passive="A court poet gains a +1 bonus to AC and on Perform checks when adjacent to two or more creatures. In addition, her movement is not impeded by crowds and she gains a bonus equal to 1/2 her skald level on Diplomacy checks to influence crowds." replace=Well-versed}

dragon_skald.name: Dragon Skald
description: ## Dragon Skald
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/19›

The Ulfen prize skalds on sea raids and linnorm hunts. Dragon skald performances involve song, whistling, or blowing mighty horns, and viking sailors often talk about having a skald along to whistle up a wind or sing away the mists on the morning of a momentous raid.

::ab[Wind Whistler (Su)]{l=1 icon=learn ability="A dragon skald adds ‹spell/alter winds›, ‹spell/fog cloud›, and ‹spell/obscuring mist› to his class spell list, at the same levels the wizard gains them. He still must add these spells to his spells known as normal." replace="Scribe Scroll"}

::ab[Sea Legs (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A dragon skald adds 1/2 his class level (minimum 1) on all Profession (sailor) checks, Survival checks while at sea, Acrobatics and Climb checks made while aboard a boat, and Swim checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Fearless Raider (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="The dragon skald becomes resistant to fear effects." passive="The dragon skald gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against fear effects, and DCs to affect the dragon skald with the Intimidate skill increase by 4." replace=Well-versed}
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head icon=info info="A dragon skald gains access to the following *raging song.*"}

::ab[Glorious Epic (Su)]{next l=3 icon=boost ability="A dragon skald learns how to truly extol the virtues and prowess of the heroic figures in his epic tales. By performing for 10 minutes and expending 1 round of *raging song,* the dragon skald can influence opinions positively towards a heroic subject of the dragon skald's choice. If the subject of the epic attempts a Diplomacy or Intimidate check against anyone who listened to the full performance, he receives a circumstance bonus on that check equal to the Will save bonus granted by the dragon skald's *inspired rage.*" replace="Song of marching"}

elegist.name: Elegist
description: ## Elegist
‹SOURCE Pathfinder Spiral of Bones #4/29›

Some skalds internalize the saddest and must mournful of stories, so much that they are able to manifest the emotions caused by these tales in physical form. These spirits are able to inflict the misery onto the elegist's enemies.

::ab[Melancholic Apparition (Su)]{icon=power head ability="By using music, oration, and similar performances, an elegist can call forth a physical manifestation of the sorrow of his tales. This functions as a ‹class/spiritualist«'s› phantom with the ‹emotionalfocus/despair› focus. He can manifest his melancholic apparition as a @HLstandard_action and dismiss it as a @HLfree_action. The ‹sidekick/phantom› appears adjacent to the skald and can act immediately. The melancholic apparition is immediately dismissed if the elegist is killed, paralyzed, stunned, knocked unconscious, or otherwise prevented from taking a @HLfree_action each round to maintain the performance that keeps the phantom manifested." usage="An elegist can keep his apparition manifested for a total number of rounds each day equal to 1 + twice his skald level + his Charisma modifier." useF="1~3~1~2+Charisma modifier" useUnit=round special="Unlike a spiritualist's phantom, a melancholic apparition can't be harbored in the elegist's consciousness. When manifested, the melancholic apparition has the same number of hit points it had when previously manifested that day. It regains all of its hit points when the elegist regains his spells for the day. If the melancholic apparition is slain, the elegist can manifest it again in the same day by spending a number of additional rounds of this ability equal to ‹misc/half› his skald level (minimum 1); the melancholic apparition returns with half its normal total of hit points." replace="Raging song and all related performances~rage powers"}

:::ab{next title=Phantom icon=power action=Ability flavor="Relevant text from the spiritualist ability." special="When the phantom is fully manifested, you can change the form of the phantom's manifestation (either from ectoplasmic to incorporeal or vice versa) as a @HLfull-round_action that provokes attacks of opportunity. A fully manifested phantom is treated as a summoned creature from the ‹rule/Ethereal Plane›, except it is not sent back to the Ethereal Plane until it is reduced to a negative amount of hit points equal to or greater than its Constitution score."}

You can manifest the phantom as an ectoplasmic or incorporeal entity. A phantom has the same alignment as you, and it can speak all the languages you can. Each phantom has an ‹ability/emotional focus› - a powerful emotion based on some experience in life that keeps it tethered to the Material and Ethereal planes. This emotional focus also grants the phantom abilities that it can use while manifested. The type of each ability and its power are determined by your level. The emotional focus determines which bonus skill ranks the phantom gains. It also determines the saving throws of the manifested phantom and the special abilities the phantom gains as it increases in level.

The phantom does not heal naturally, and can be healed only with magic or by being tended to with the Heal skill while fully manifested in ectoplasmic form. If the phantom is banished to the Ethereal Plane, it can't manifest again for 24 hours.
While fully manifested, a phantom can't be returned to the Ethereal Plane by means of ‹spell/dispel magic›, but spells such as ‹spell/dismissal› and ‹spell/banishment› work normally.

Fully manifested phantoms can wear armor and use items (though not wield weapons) appropriate to their forms. Any items worn, carried, or held by a phantom are dropped when the phantom demanifests, and must be retrieved and donned anew if the phantom wishes to use them when it fully manifests in the future.

Phantoms are self-involved, even jealous, creatures that do not play well with other summoned companions. A phantom refuses to manifest in the presence of an eidolon or shadow you have summoned. Furthermore, if a phantom is manifested when such a creature is summoned, it immediately retreats and will not manifest again until the eidolon or shadow is dismissed.
A fully manifested phantom's abilities, feats, Hit Dice, saving throws, and skills are tied to your class level and increase as you gain levels.

::ab[Despair]{flavor="Relevant text from the emotional focus." next icon=boost-def-lower order=npa info="Despair phantoms often appear twisted or wounded, showing the grisly circumstances of their demise. Their coloration tends to have a grayish or sickly green cast. When they speak, they do so in terrifying whispers or high-pitched screeches." passive="The phantom gains a number of ranks in Intimidate and Stealth equal to its number of Hit Dice. Its ‹misc/good saving throws› are Fortitude and Will. The phantom gains a +2 bonus on attack and damage rolls against creatures that are shaken, frightened, panicked, cowering, or subject to effects such as *aura of despair* or *crushing despair.*" ability="*&quot&Miserable Strike&quot&* - If the phantom hits a creature with a slam attack, that creature must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the phantom's Hit Dice + the phantom's Charisma modifier) or take a -2 penalty on attack and damage rolls for 1 round. This is a mind-affecting fear and emotion effect. Penalties from multiple hits don't stack with themselves." imp7="As a @HLswift_action, the phantom can emit a 10-foot *aura of despair.* Enemies within the aura take a -2 penalty on all saving throws. This is a fear effect. Deactivating the aura is a @HLfree_action." imp12="Three times per day as a @HLstandard_action, the phantom can emit a shout that acts as ‹spell/crushing despair›. The phantom uses its Hit Dice as its caster level for the effect, and the DC of the effect equals 10 + 1/2 the phantom's Hit Dice + the phantom's Charisma modifier." imp17="If the phantom hits with its slam attack, the creature hit doesn't get a save to resist the effects of *miserable strike.*"}
::ab[Steady Hearted (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="An elegist receives a +4 bonus to saving throws against emotion effects." replace=Well-versed}
::ab[Somber Damage Reduction (Ex)]{icon=def l9="The damage reduction an elegist gains at 9th level is shared by his melancholic apparition when it is manifested." alter="Damage reduction"}

::ab[Master Elegist (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="The elegist can manifest his melancholic apparition as a @HLswift_action. If it is slain. the elegist can manifest a new phantom in the same day with no additional expenditure of rounds of the melancholic apparition ability, and the new phantom has all of its hit points."}

fated_champion.name: Fated Champion
description: ## Fated Champion
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/114›

Many cultures see fate as a limit that is both stifling and unwanted, regardless of the destiny that lurks in the days and years ahead. Among cultures where skalds are the keepers of lore and wisdom, there are those who learn to read the winds of fate and take up the mantle of the fated champion, knowing and embracing their destined paths with strength borne of conviction.

::ab[Watcher of the Weave (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost flavor="A fated champion learns to see the tapestry of events moments before it is woven." passive="He gains an insight bonus on initiative checks equal to 1/2 his skald level." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Far Seer (Su)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="A fated champion learns a method of *spell kenning* that is especially suited for divining and understanding the future, but not for directly harming opponents. For the purpose of determining what level spell slot he expends when using *spell kenning,* the skald treats the spell level of spells that predict the future (such as ‹spell/augury› and ‹spell/divination›) as one spell level lower (minimum spell level 1st), and treats the spell level of spells that deal damage as one spell level higher." alter="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Shield of Foresight (Su)]{l=10 icon=def flavor="A fated champion's certainty of his own fate grants him the ability to stare down fear and remain unbowed." passive="When using *raging song,* he is immune to fear effects, and allies affected by his *raging song* gain a +5 bonus on saves attempted against fear effects." replace="Dirge of doom *raging song*"}

::ab[Not This Day (Su)]{l=20 icon=roll flavor="The fated champion gains the ability to reweave the strands of fate." immediate="He can expend 10 rounds of *raging song* to either reroll a saving throw or force an opponent to reroll an attack roll. The decision to use this ability must be made before the results of the initial roll are revealed, and the champion or his opponent must take the result of the reroll." replace="Master skald"}

::list[The following rage powers complement the fated champion archetype]{hl link=ragepower all="boasting taunt~clear mind~eater of magic~fierce fortitude~flesh wound~guarded life~intimidating glare~cairn linnorm death curse~tarn linnorm death curse~renewed vitality"}

herald_of_the_horn.name: Herald of the Horn
description: ## Herald of the Horn
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/115›

Even the loudest voice can often times be drowned out by the din of battle. Whether with the polished metal trumpet of a standing army or the crude curved animal horn of savage raiders, a herald of the horn sounds his *raging song* with thunderous blasts, which can bolster allies or shatter castle walls.

::ab[Arcane Bond (Ex)]{l=1 head icon=power ability="A herald of the horn forms a powerful bond with a horn (‹eq-misc/mwk_>musical instrument›). This functions like an ‹sorcbloodline/arcane› bloodline ‹class/sorcerer«'s› arcane bond with an object. Like a weapon, wand, or staff, the horn must be held in one hand when the herald of the horn casts skald spells." replace="Scribe Scroll"}

::ab[Arcane]{next icon=magic flavor="Relevant text from the sorcerer bloodline." ability="Your sorcerer levels stack with any wizard levels you possess when determining the powers of your bonded object. Once per day, your bonded item allows you to cast any one of your spells known (unlike a wizard's bonded item, which allows him to cast any one spell in his spellbook)."}
:::ab{next title="Bonded Object" action=Ability flavor="Relevant text from the wizard ability." icon=power-magic}

You begin play with a bonded object at no cost. This object is always masterwork quality. If you attempt to cast a spell without your bonded object in hand, you must make a ‹rule/concentration› check or lose the spell. The DC for this check is equal to 20 + the spell's level.

A bonded object can be used once per day to cast any one spell that you have in your spellbook and are capable of casting, even if the spell is not prepared. This spell is treated like any other spell cast by you, including casting time, duration, and other effects dependent on your level. This spell cannot be modified by metamagic feats or other abilities.

You can add additional magic abilities to your bonded object as if you have the required item creation feats and if you meet the level prerequisites of the feat. For example, a wizard with a bonded dagger must be at least 5th level to add magic abilities to the dagger (see ‹feat/Craft Magic Arms and Armor›). The magic properties of a bonded object, including any magic abilities added to the object, only function for the wizard who owns it. If a bonded object's owner dies, or the item is replaced, the object reverts to being an ordinary masterwork item of the appropriate type.

If a bonded object is damaged, it is restored to full hit points the next time you prepare your spells. If the object of an arcane bond is lost or destroyed, it can be replaced after 1 week in a special ritual that costs 200 gp per wizard level plus the cost of the masterwork item. This ritual takes 8 hours to complete. Items replaced in this way do not possess any of the additional enchantments of the previous bonded item. You can designate an existing magic item as your bonded item. This functions in the same way as replacing a lost or destroyed item except that the new magic item retains its abilities while gaining the benefits and drawbacks of becoming a bonded item.

::ab[Rousing Retort (Su)]{l=5 icon=def flavor="A herald of the horn can use *raging song* to free allies from enchantment effects and fear." ability="When beginning a *raging song,* he can expend 4 rounds of that ability to grant all allies within 60 feet a new saving throw against an ongoing enchantment or fear effect. The allies gain a +2 bonus on this new saving throw." replace="The first daily use of spell kenning gained at 5th level"}

::ab[Horn Call (Su)]{l=7 icon=boost flavor="A herald's horn enhances his sonic spells." passive="If a skald spell with the sonic descriptor is cast using the horn, its DC increases by 1." incrementPlain="The DC now increases~7~6" incrementDesc=by replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Crumbling Blast (Su)]{icon=magic flavor="A herald of the horn can use his horn to create a devastating shock wave of energy." l11="Once per day, he can sound a note on the horn that functions like a ‹magic-wondrous/horn of blasting› (DC = 10 + 1/2 the herald of the horn's level + his Charisma bonus)." l17="The herald of the horn can use this ability twice per day and it functions like a ‹magic-wondrous/greater horn of blasting›." special="Unlike an actual horn of blasting, this ability does not carry the risk of destroying the horn." replace="The second and third daily uses of spell kenning gained at 11th and 17th levels"}

::list[The following rage powers complement the herald of the horn archetype]{hl link=ragepower all="battle roar~energy absorption~energy eruption~fearless rage~knockback~knockdown~smasher~swift foot"}

hunt_caller.name: Hunt Caller
description: ## Hunt Caller
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/85›

Hunt callers are skalds whose songs draw their allies into the animal world. They can invoke the inner beasts of those who observe their supernatural performances - a talent that causes many to mistake the hunt caller and her allies for lycanthropes or other shapechanging creatures.
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head icon=power info="A hunt caller gains access to the following *raging songs.*"}

::ab[Song of the Senses (Su)]{next l=2 icon=power-boost flavor="The hunt caller's *raging song* unlocks animalistic senses in her allies." ability="By spending 1 round of *raging song,* the hunt caller can affect all allies within 60 feet for 1 hour. The allies gain low-light vision and a +2 competence bonus on Perception and Survival checks. If an ally already has low-light vision, the range doubles. The skald must continue to perform the song for the remainder of the hour; otherwise, its effects end, but only 1 round of *raging song* is expended for that hour." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Call of the Wild (Su)]{next l=6 icon=magic ability="The hunt caller's *raging song* can transform herself and her allies within 60 feet into animalistic shapes, as per ‹spell/beast shape I› (Small animals only) at her caster level. The same transformation applies to all targets. By spending 1 round of *raging song* per target, the hunt caller can affect any number of allies within 60 feet for 1 hour. When in animal form, the hunt caller is treated as able to speak normally for the purpose of using *raging song,* but not for using other abilities that require speech (such as spellcasting). The skald must continue to perform the song for the remainder of the hour; otherwise, its effects end, but only 1 round of *raging song* per target is expended for that hour." imp10="The hunt caller can instead choose a Medium animal shape." imp14="The hunt caller can choose ‹spell/shapechanger's gift› or the effects of ‹spell/beast shape II›." imp18="She can choose the effects of ‹spell/beast shape III›." replace="Song of strength~song of the fallen~rage power gained at 18th level"}

::ab[Wilderness Magic (Su)]{icon=learn l5="The hunt caller adds two spells of any level she can cast from the ‹main/druid spell« list› to her class spell list and list of skald spells known." repeat="She adds two more spells.~11~6" replace="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Inspire Scent (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power ability="The hunt caller gains the ‹ragepower/scent› rage power. Her *song of the senses* and *inspired rage raging songs* both grant affected allies this rage power." replace="Rage power gained at 6th level"}

::list[The following rage powers complement the hunt caller archetype]{hl link=ragepower all="animal fury~battle roar~beast totem|lesser, normal, and greater~intimidating glare~low-light vision~no escape~primal scent~quick reflexes~sprint~swift foot"}

instigator.name: Instigator
description: ## Instigator
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/49›

The instigator influences the will of the people, turning them toward his own purposes. Regardless of whether what the instigator says is true or not, he inflames emotions in others and suspends their reason. Instigators are often revolutionaries, political or otherwise, but they might also be warmongering firebrands or wicked souls who spread prejudice against others.
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head icon=power info="An instigator gains the following *raging songs.*"}

::ab[Song of Rabble-Rousing (Sp)]{next l=5 icon=magic ability="The instigator can perform for 1 full round and spend 4 rounds of *raging song.* This affects those who hear the song for up to 1 hour as per ‹spell/enthrall›." replace="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Song of Riot (Sp, Su)]{next l=10 icon=magic flavor="The instigator can fill the hearts of his listeners with hatred and rouse them into a frenzy." ability="He can use this performance on all targets with fewer than 4 Hit Dice and a Wisdom score lower than 16 who are currently *enthralled,* without disrupting the *enthrall* effect (even if a creature succeeds at its saving throw). This ability requires a @HLstandard_action to activate and costs 4 additional rounds of *raging song.*" special="Enthralled creatures must each attempt a new saving throw against an effect that functions as the ‹spell/foster hatred› spell, except that, unlike with the spell, the instigator is free to choose subgroups, organizations, religions, or the like regardless of the targets' races. Creatures that fail the saving throw or choose to forego it also gain the effects of the instigator's *raging song* whenever they attack the target of their hatred, for as long as the *foster hatred* effect lasts; however, they do not gain the effects of the instigator's rage powers when they're under the effects of *raging song* in this way." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Revolutionary Heart (Su)]{l=7 icon=def flavor="An instigator's tumultuous mastery of demagoguery and revolution makes it difficult for others' compulsions to sway him." ability="Once per day, when he fails a saving throw against a compulsion effect, he can attempt another saving throw after 1 time increment (1 round if the effect is measured in rounds, 1 minute if the effect is measured in minutes, 10 minutes if the spell is measured in 10-minute increments, and so on)." replace="Lore master" incrementUse="The skald~7~6"}

red_tongue.name: Red Tongue
description: ## Red Tongue
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/26›

In the young tengu nation of Kwanlai, politicos argue endlessly over how best to rule and guide a people who have been parasites and vassals to other nations for millennia. The tengu tendency toward dramatic flourishes and rhetoric only complicates this debate, creating an environment in which political clubs led by hot-blooded firebrands - referred to as red tongues in polite company - dominate the political scene by swaying emotions in the moment and wielding magic in the shadows. Many political debates in Hisuikarasu end in violent brawls or daring duels.
::prereq{r=Tengu}

::ab[Great Orator]{icon=down info="A red tongue can activate his *raging song* performances only by using the Perform (oratory) skill." imp2="He must select Perform (oratory) as his versatile performance choice." alter="Raging song~versatile performance"}

::ab[Seed of Discord (Su)]{flavor="The fiery outlook of the red tongue imparts instinctual knowledge to invest arcane energy into his proclamations and denouncements." icon=learn ability="The red tongue gains the following bonus spells known at the listed spell level as he reaches the appropriate level to cast each spell." s1=Doom s2=Castigate s3="Charm monster" s4=Denounce s5="Greater command" s6="Mass eagle's splendor"}

::ab[Rile (Ex)]{flavor="The red tongue is particularly skilled at provoking others to action, even when ignorant on a subject." icon=boost passive="He adds 1/2 his skald level on Bluff checks to deceive or conceal his motives and on Intimidate checks to improve a creature's attitude." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Rogue Talents (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="The red tongue learns a ‹ability/rogue talent› of his choice, treating his skald level as his rogue level. He cannot select a talent that requires or modifies a class feature he does not have (such as sneak attack), and he must meet all other prerequisites." repeat="He gains a new rogue talent, following the rules above.~12~5" replace="Versatile performance selections normally gained at 7th, 12th, and 17th levels"}

::ab[Duplicitous Rhetoric (Su)]{l=7 icon=power ability="When a red tongue begins a *raging song,* he can select a single rogue talent he knows and grant it to all creatures under the influence of his *inspired rage.*" special="If a rogue talent is limited to a certain number of uses per day, each creature affected is limited to a single use and cannot gain additional uses from a *raging song* for 24 hours, even if the red tongue ends his *raging song* and starts again."}

serpent_herald.name: Serpent Herald
description: ## Serpent Herald
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/28›

Warrior bands devoted to ‹faith/Ragadahn› are often led by a serpent herald, whose deep roars shake the earth itself. Rumors tell of waves of snakes slithering forth from their nests in awe as these bellowing commanders take on serpentine features.

:::ab{l=3 icon=boost-def-power-melee action=Ability title="Serpentine Rage (Su)" replace="Rage power gained at 3rd level" order=Cn info="The serpent herald must have the *inspired rage raging song* to use this ability."}

A serpent herald can call down Ragadahn's features onto his allies. Allies affected by the serpent herald's *inspired rage* ability gain a serpentine aspect of the serpent herald's choice (all affected allies gain the same aspect). The serpent herald may choose from the following aspects:
- *Movement* (+8 competence bonus on Climb and Swim checks, and targets can take 10 on these checks even when rushed or threatened)
- *Scales* (+2 natural armor bonus)
- *Senses* (low-light vision and ‹umr/scent›)
- *Natural bite attack* (1d4 damage [for a Medium creature](umr/natural_attack), plus poison).
::aff[Poison]{iconP type=Bite-injury dcF dcLev="serpent herald" dcIt=his dcMod=Con freqR=6 effCon=1 cure1}

This is a polymorph effect; a creature under another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape,* does not gain the aspect. If the serpent herald grants one of these aspects to himself, he also gains the ability to ‹spell/speak with animals› (reptiles and creatures with the aquatic subtype only) for as long as he has the aspect.

::ab[Serpent Shape (Su)]{l=5 head icon=magic ability="A serpent herald gains the ability to use *wild shape* to take the form of a reptile or an aquatic animal once per day, as the ‹class/druid› class feature. His effective druid level for this ability is equal to his serpent herald level - 1. This doesn't allow him to assume other forms, such as elementals or other kinds of animals. When in animal form, the serpent herald is treated as able to speak normally for the purpose of using *raging song,* but not for using other abilities that require speech (such as spellcasting)." incrementUse="A serpent herald~5~6" imp11="He gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Wild Speech› feat while using this ability, treating his druid level as his serpent herald level - 1." imp17="In addition, he gains the supernatural resiliency of a ‹family/linnorm›, gaining the effects of ‹spell/freedom of movement› while using this ability." replace="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{next flavor="Relevant text from the druid class ability." icon=power provokes=No standard="You gain the ability to turn yourself into any Small or Medium animal and back again. Your options for new forms include all creatures with the ‹main/monsters_>animal<s› type. This ability functions like the ‹spell/beast shape I› spell, except as noted here. The effect lasts for 1 hour per druid level, or until you change back. The form chosen must be that of an animal with which you are familiar." special="You lose your ability to speak while in animal form because you are limited to the sounds that a normal, untrained animal can make, but you can communicate normally with other animals of the same general grouping as your new form." imp6="You can *wild shape* twice a day as ‹spell/beast shape II›, and you can now change into a Large or Tiny animal." imp8="You can *wild shape* 3 times/day as ‹spell/beast shape III›, and you can now change into a Huge or Diminutive animal."}

::list[The following rage powers complement the serpent herald archetype]{hl link=ragepower all="animal fury~battle roar~knockback~knockdown~linnorm death curse|any~low-light vision~sprint~swift foot"}

spell_warrior.name: Spell Warrior
description: ## Spell Warrior
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/116›

The spell warrior uses his arcane knowledge rather than his rage to turn the tide of battle in favor of himself and his allies. With a clash of bracers and a sonorous chant, the Spell Warrior's song reaches out to touch the weapons of his allies, lending them arcane power. While his song does not inspire rage, he can use the power of his music to enchant the weapons of his allies and counter the spells of his foes.
::ab[Improved Counterspell]{l=1 icon=power ability="The spell warrior receives the ‹feat/Improved Counterspell› feat as a bonus feat." replace="Scribe Scroll"}
::ab[Weapon Song (Su)]{head icon=power ability="A spell warrior gains the following *raging song,* allowing him to grant his ally's weapons enhancement bonuses and special powers."}

:::ab{next title="Enhance Weapons (Su)" action=Ability l=1 icon=boost replace="Inspired rage" special="The bonus and special abilities granted by this *raging song* are determined when the song begins, and cannot be changed until the *raging song* ends and another is begun. These bonuses apply to only one end of a double weapon."}

The spell warrior can grant an enhancement bonus to the weapons (including ammunition) of allies within 60 feet. This bonus is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of his skald level. The maximum bonus gained is based upon the number of weapons affected: +5 to one weapon, +4 to two weapons, +3 to three weapons, or +2 to four or more weapons. Fifty pieces of ammunition count as one weapon for this purpose. The wielder of a weapon enhanced by this *raging song* counts as if he were under the effect of an *inspired rage raging song* for all purposes involving the skald's rage powers.

These bonuses can also be used to add any of the following weapon special abilities to the weapons enhanced by this ability: ‹magic-enh/dancing›, ‹magic-enh/defending›, ‹magic-enh/distance›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/frost›, ‹magic-enh/ghost touch›, ‹magic-enh/keen›, ‹magic-enh/mighty cleaving›, ‹magic-enh/returning›, ‹magic-enh/shock›, ‹magic-enh/seeking›, or ‹magic-enh/speed›. Adding these weapon special abilities consumes an amount of bonus equal to the [special ability's cost](main/magic_enhancements). These enhancement bonuses and special abilities overlap with any enhancements or special abilities the weapon already has, though duplicate special abilities do not stack. If an affected weapon is not magical, at least a +1 enhancement bonus must be added before any other special abilities can be.

::ab[Greater Counterspell (Su)]{icon=boost flavor="The spell warrior gains increased versatility when attempting to counteract enemy spellcasting." l5="When counterspelling, he can expend two skald spell slots of the same level or higher than the opponent's spell, regardless of the school of that spell." l11="When counterspelling an opponent's spell, the spell warrior only has to expend one skald spell slot of the level of the spell being cast." l17="The spell warrior gains ‹feat/Parry Spell› as a bonus feat. The skald does not have to meet the prerequisites for this feat." replace="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Song of Arcane Manipulation (Su)]{l=10 icon=magic flavor="A spell warrior can sacrifice his own rage magic to counter an opponent's spell." immediate="When using *raging song,* he can counterspell without interrupting his *raging song.* However, in addition to expending a spell slot (or spell slots) to attempt to counter the opponent's spell, the skald must expend 1 round of *raging song* per spell level of the opponent's spell (for example, if attempting to counterspell a 3rd-level spell, the skald must expend one of his own 3rd-level spell slots and 3 rounds of *raging song*)." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Spell Tamper (Su)]{l=20 icon=magic ability="When a spell warrior successfully counterspells an opponent's spell, the opponent suffers a backlash of magical energy and takes 1d6 points of damage per spell level of the countered spell. If the opponent succeeds at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the skald's level + the skald's Charisma modifier), the damage is reduced by half. This damage is magical and is not subject to damage reduction or energy resistance." replace="Master skald"}

::list[The following rage powers complement the spell warrior archetype]{hl link=ragepower all="clear mind~disruptive~eater of magic~elemental rage|lesser, normal, and greater~energy resistance~spell sunder~spellbreaker~sunder enchantment~superstition~witch hunter"}

sunsinger.name: Sunsinger
description: ## Sunsinger
‹SOURCE Qadira, Jewel of the East/33›
Qadiran sunsingers are particularly religious skalds of Sarenrae who call down their goddess's glory to fill soldiers with fire.
::prereq{other="Must be lawful good, neutral good, or neutral, and must worship ‹faith/Sarenrae›"}

::ab[Pillar of Light (Su)]{l=3 icon=power flavor="A sunsinger skald can use her *raging song* to call upon her goddess to imbue her with glory and make all who see it pay heed." ability="A great beam of sunlight shines down upon the skald, casting ‹rule/bright light› in a 30-foot radius, and allows the skald's *raging song* to function as the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance." replace="Song of marching"}

::ab[Channel Solar Energy (Su)]{l=5 icon=aura ability="A sunsinger skald can ‹ability/channel energy› as a cleric once per day to heal wounds or harm undead like a good-aligned cleric. When she does so, she fills the area affected by the channeled energy with light, and can outline creatures in the area of effect as per ‹spell/faerie fire›. The sunsinger uses her skald level as her effective cleric level when channeling positive energy." special="Undead who are particularly harmed by sunlight take a -2 penalty on saving throws against this channeled energy, and take 1 additional point of damage per die of damage dealt by the effect." incrementUse="A sunsinger~5~6" replace="Spell kenning"}

totem_channeler.name: Totem Channeler
description: ## Totem Channeler
‹SOURCE Disciple's Doctrine/24›
Some Shoanti skalds discover deeper truths in their tribes' totems. These skalds, known as totem channelers, learn how to share the totems' gifts with their allies in the heat of battle.

::ab[Totemic Versatility (Ex)]{icon=power choice="When a totem channeler learns a new rage power, he can select from more than one group of ‹arc-barbarian/totem rage power«s›, even though he normally could not do so. Totem rage powers grant benefits related to a theme, are organized in groups of three, and usually have the word &quot&totem&quot& in their name." special="When the totem channeler starts an *inspired rage raging song,* he can add totem rage powers from only one group at a time. He can change the group of added totem rage powers by ending the current *inspired rage* and starting a new one." replace="Bardic knowledge" alter="Rage powers"}

::ab[Tandem Totems (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="When a totem channeler starts an *inspired rage raging song* and adds a totem rage power, he can add totem rage powers from a different group. Adding or maintaining totem rage powers from the additional group requires spending 1 additional round of *raging song* each round." imp11="He can activate up to two additional totem groups." imp17="He can add up to three additional totem groups." special="He must spend 1 additional round of *raging song* each round for each additional totem group. If the totem channeler does not have enough rounds of *raging song* remaining to maintain his *inspired rage,* the song ends." replace="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Totem's Guidance (Su)]{l=9 icon=boost flavor="The totem channeler can draw strength and protection from his totem powers when he wades into battle with song on his lips." ability="When he adds a totem rage power to his *inspired rage,* he and his allies gain a +1 insight bonus to AC and on saving throws for the duration of the totem channeler's *raging song.*" imp14="This bonus increases to +2." special="This bonus applies only to the first totem rage power he applies to his *inspired rage.*" replace="Damage reduction"}

totemic_skald.name: Totemic Skald
description: ## Totemic Skald
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/117›

The totemic skald forms a close connection to an animal totem. Through the power of this mystical ally, the skald can change shapes, assuming its form as his own. Additionally, the totem animal grants the skald a measure of its spiritual power that he can share with his allies.

::ab[Totem (Su)]{head l=3 icon=power choice="The totemic skald chooses one animal from the hunter's ‹ability/animal focus› list. Once selected, this choice cannot be changed. This animal becomes the skald's personal totem animal and influences his later abilities. He gains the following rage power."}

::ab[Song of the Beast (Su)]{next icon=boost ability="The totemic skald grants the *animal focus* abilities of his totem animal (as the hunter's *animal focus* ability) to all allies affected by his *raging song.* He treats his skald level as his hunter level for determining the abilities of the animal focus (such as the improvements gained at 8th and 15th level)." replace="Rage power gained at 3rd level"}

::ab[Totem Empathy (Ex and Sp)]{l=4 icon=power order=a2 ability="The totemic skald can improve the attitude of animals of the same type as his totem. This ability functions just like a Diplomacy check to improve the attitude of a person. The skald rolls 1d20 and adds his skald level and his Charisma modifier to determine the totem empathy check result. The typical domestic animal has a starting attitude of indifferent, while wild animals are usually unfriendly." ability2="In addition, the totemic skald can cast ‹spell/charm animal› as a spell-like ability, using his skald level as his caster level and his Charisma modifier to determine the DC. He can do this a number of times a day equal to ‹misc/one-fourth› of his skald level. This only functions against animals that are of the same type as his totem animal (subject to GM discretion)." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=5 head icon=magic ability="A totemic skald gains the ability to *wild shape* into the form of a Small or Medium version of his totem animal, as the ‹class/druid› class feature. His effective druid level for this ability is equal to his skald level - 1." incrementUse=He~5~6 special="This doesn't allow the skald to assume other forms, such as elementals, plants, or other kinds of animals. When in animal form, the skald is treated as able to speak normally for the purpose of using *raging song,* but not for using other abilities that require speech (such as spellcasting). The skald uses his class level as his druid level for the purpose of qualifying for feats that affect wild shape (such as ‹feat/Wild Speech›)." replace="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{next flavor="Relevant text from the druid class ability." icon=power provokes=No standard="You gain the ability to turn yourself into any Small or Medium animal and back again. Your options for new forms include all creatures with the ‹main/monsters_>animal<s› type. This ability functions like the ‹spell/beast shape I› spell, except as noted here. The effect lasts for 1 hour per druid level, or until you change back. The form chosen must be that of an animal with which you are familiar." special="You lose your ability to speak while in animal form because you are limited to the sounds that a normal, untrained animal can make, but you can communicate normally with other animals of the same general grouping as your new form. (The normal sound a wild parrot makes is a squawk, so changing to this form does not permit speech.)" imp6="You can *wild shape* twice a day as ‹spell/beast shape II›, and you can now change into a Large or Tiny animal." imp8="You can *wild shape* 3 times/day as ‹spell/beast shape III›, and you can now change into a Huge or Diminutive animal."}

::list[The following rage powers complement the totemic skald archetype]{hl link=ragepower all="animal fury~beast totem|lesser, normal, and greater~knockback~knockdown~low-light vision~primal scent~scent~sprint~swift foot"}

twilight_speaker.name: Twilight Speaker
description: ## Twilight Speaker
‹SOURCE Heroes from the Fringe/12›

From the extraordinarily reclusive Snowcaster elves come the twilight speakers, who consider it a sacred duty to seek out and befriend non-elven communities. These elves travel among outsiders as envoys, diplomats, traders, and even spies as events demand. Deeply devoted to the elven goddess Findeladlara, twilight speakers wander far and wide, vigilant for threats to their homes and their people.
::prereq{r=Elf}

::ab[Devout]{icon=warning ability="A twilight speaker must worship the elven goddess ‹faith/Findeladlara›, and his alignment must be within one step of chaotic good. A twilight speaker who does not meet these requirements cannot use *inspired devotion* or *Findeladlara's blessing.*"}
::ab[Twilight Envoy]{icon=boost passive="A twilight speaker gains a bonus equal to half his skald level on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head icon=power info="A twilight speaker gains the following *raging songs.*"}

::ab[Inspired Devotion (Su)]{next icon=boost-def flavor="The twilight speaker inspires fervor rather than fury." ability="Affected allies gain a competence bonus on attack rolls and a morale bonus on saving throws. Both of these bonuses are equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-sixth› of his skald level."}

::ab[Song of Understanding (Su)]{next l=6 icon=aura-power ability="A twilight speaker can use his *raging song* to create an aura that allows creatures to speak and understand each others' languages. By expending 4 rounds of *raging song,* the twilight speaker creates an aura that grants him and creatures in a 60-foot ‹misc/spread› centered on him the effects of ‹spell/tongues›. This aura uses the skald's level as the caster level for all purposes, including duration. A twilight speaker can dismiss this aura as a @HLstandard_action."}

::ab[Song of Secrecy (Su)]{next l=10 icon=boost ability="A twilight speaker can use his song to shroud allies from detection. Affected allies gain a bonus equal to half the twilight speaker's skald level on Stealth checks, and they can attempt such checks without ‹rule/cover› or ‹rule/concealment›. Song of secrecy is audible only to those affected." replace="Inspired rage~song of strength~dirge of doom"}

::ab[Community Domain]{l=2 icon=magic-learn ability="A twilight speaker gains the ‹domain/Community› domain and gains the powers and spells of the domain. He treats his skald level as his cleric level, and he uses his Charisma modifier in place of his Wisdom modifier for the purposes of the domain's abilities." replace="Versatile performance~well-versed"}

::ab[Findeladlara's Blessing (Su)]{l=7 icon=boost ability="Once per day a twilight speaker can activate any spell trigger or spell completion item as though the spell were on the skald spell list." incrementUse=He~7~6 replace="Lore master"}
::ab[Findeladlara's Hand]{l=20 icon=boost ability="The twilight speaker can use *Findeladlara's blessing* at will." replace="Master skald"}

undying_word.name: Undying Word
description: ## Undying Word
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/5›
Some who have survived the Mana Wastes cannot accept defeat, and those who have learned to speak the undying word can lend others the strength to endure.

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=1 icon=power ability="An undying word gains a bonus feat in addition to those gained from normal advancement. He must meet the prerequisites for the feat, and it must include Endurance as a prerequisite or be selected from the following list: ‹feat/Endurance›, ‹feat/Great Fortitude›, or ‹feat/Improved Great Fortitude›." repeat="He gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above.~7~6" replace="Scribe scroll~lore master"}
::ab[Undying Song (Su)]{head icon=info info="An undying word gains the following *raging songs,* granting his allies the ability to withstand punishment."}

::ab[Inspire Resilience (Su)]{next l=1 icon=down-def ability="The undying word can grant his companions supernatural endurance. This functions as *inspired rage,* but it grants neither a bonus to his allies' Strength nor a -1 penalty to their AC." replace="Inspired rage"}

::ab[Song of Defiance (Su)]{next l=6 icon=magic flavor="An undying word can shield his allies against the environment." ability="By expending 1 round of *raging song,* the undying word grants all allies within 60 feet the effects of ‹spell/endure elements› for the next hour. The undying word must continue to perform for the remainder of the hour; otherwise its effects end, but only 1 round of *raging song* is expended for that hour." replace="Song of strength"}

::ab[Dirge of Determination (Su)]{next l=10 icon=aid flavor="An undying word can urge his allies within 30 feet to overcome hardships." passive="Affected allies treat ‹rule/ability damage«, ability drain›, and penalties to ability scores as though each were 2 lower." incrementPlain="This reduction~10~5~4~2" special="The undying word can never reduce a penalty below 0 or increase an ability score beyond its original value in this manner." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Endurance Power (Su)]{l=5 icon=power ability="An undying word learns an additional rage power that affects him and any allies under the influence of his *inspire resilience.* This rage power must be selected from the following list: ‹ragepower/clear mind›, ‹ragepower/energy absorption›, ‹ragepower/energy resistance›, ‹ragepower/flesh wound›, ‹ragepower/greater energy resistance›, ‹ragepower/greater guarded life›, ‹ragepower/guarded life›, ‹ragepower/increased damage reduction›, ‹ragepower/internal fortitude›, or ‹ragepower/superstition›. This otherwise functions as the skald's *rage powers* ability." repeat="He learns another rage power, following the restrictions above.~11~6" replace="Spell kenning"}

urban_skald.name: Urban Skald
description: ## Urban Skald
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Streets/25›
The urban skald finds that challenging and mocking foes is sometimes more effective than inspiring uncontrolled rage in a city.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down info="An urban skald is not proficient with medium armor." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head icon=info info="An urban skald gains a different selection of *raging songs,* as noted below. The save DC for a *raging song* that allows a save is equal to 10 + 1/2 the urban skald's skald level + his Charisma modifier."}

::ab[Controlled Inspired Rage (Su)]{next icon=boost ability="When the urban skald *inspires rage,* he does not grant the normal benefits. Instead, he can apply a +2 morale bonus to his allies' Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution." increment="The bonus~0~8~4~2" special="The urban skald can apply the full bonus to one ability score or split the bonus between several scores in increments of 2. The choice applies to all affected allies. The controlled *inspired rage* grants no bonus on Will saves, imposes no penalties to AC, and does not prevent affected allies from using Intelligence-, Dexterity-, or Charisma-based skills." alter="Inspired rage"}

::ab[Infuriating Mockery (Su)]{next l=3 icon=lower ability="The urban skald can inspire reckless fury in one foe within 30 feet. If the foe fails a Will saving throw, it takes a -2 penalty to AC and on attack rolls, cannot use any Intelligence-, Dexterity-, or Charisma-based skills, and must succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check to cast spells (DC = 15 + spell level) for as long as it remains in range of the skald and the performance is maintained." repeat="The urban skald can target an additional foe with this ability.~7~4" replace="Song of marching~damage reduction"}

::ab[Humiliating Defamation (Su)]{next l=10 icon=lower order=ap ability="The urban skald can ostracize a foe. The song has one primary target, who the skald must see and name or describe. That target must attempt a Will save. On a failed save, the primary target cannot benefit from any morale bonus or teamwork feats, cannot treat any creature as an ally, and must attempt to save against any spell cast on it (including harmless spells, even if it casts them on itself). These effects remain as long as the primary target can hear the skald and the skald maintains the performance." passive="All other foes within 60 feet of the skald are secondary targets. Secondary targets must succeed at Will saves or they cannot treat the primary target as an ally, and cannot target it with spells or abilities that are harmless. These effects remain as long as the secondary targets are within 60 feet of the skald and the skald maintains the performance." special="This is a sonic mind-affecting charm effect." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Back of the Crowd (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def flavor="An urban skald has learned to maximize the defensive benefit of being near allies." passive="He gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC when adjacent to 2 or more allies." increment="This bonus~3~6"}

war_painter.name: War Painter
description: ## War Painter
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/10›
The gripplis of the Valashmai Jungle exhibit savage strength for their size - attributed in part to the frightful magical pigments applied to their skin by the tribes' mystical lore keepers.
::prereq{r=Grippli}

::ab[Furious Paint (Su)]{icon=power order=af ability="By spending 10 minutes preparing and applying special paints to his or an ally's skin, a war painter can create patterns that store the effects of one of his *raging songs.* The war painter must expend 1 or more rounds of his *raging song* class ability while anointing the ally, and the paint retains its potency until the war painter recovers his daily uses of *raging song.* The ally can activate the paint as a @HLmove_action, gaining the benefits of the *raging song,* including any rage powers as appropriate. This effect lasts a number of rounds equal to the rounds of *raging song* the war painter expended + the war painter's ranks in ‹skill/Craft› (calligraphy, paintings, or tattoos) - but no more than double the number of rounds of *raging song* expended." free="By halving the remaining number of rounds of *raging song,* an ally can suspend the paint's effects and activate the paint again later as a @HLmove_action." special="A war painter can apply the effects of any feats that affect ‹ability/bardic performance› to his furious paint, except effects that extend the effects of performances, such as the ‹feat/Lingering Performance› feat. A creature can be subject to only one application of furious paint at a time. The war painter can still use his *raging song* ability in the normal way." alter="Raging song"}

::ab[Thousand Totems (Su)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A war painter can channel a powerful totem into his furious paints while applying the pigments on an ally. This grants the painted ally the benefits of one additional rage power when the ally activates the furious paints. The war painter does not need to know the rage power, but it must be one for which he qualifies. Furthermore, the war painter can select only ‹ability/a blood »rage power<s› or a ‹arc-barbarian/totem rage power›. If the war painter already has a different blood rage or totem rage power that would be applied to the painted ally, this new rage power replaces it and any other rage powers that require it as a prerequisite." usage="Once per day" incrementUse=He~5~6 special="He can apply this ability multiple times to the same creature, though only to give it rage powers that use the granted rage power as a prerequisite (such as ‹ragepower/beast totem› and ‹ragepower/greater beast totem›)." replace="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Arcane Flourish (Su)]{l=7 icon=magic ability="Once per day as he finishes applying his *furious paint* to a creature, a war painter can cast one skald spell with a casting time of no more than 1 standard action and infuse the spell into the paint. Once while benefiting from the paint's *raging song,* the painted ally can use a @HLstandard_action to cast the infused spell on herself as if the war painter had cast it. The spell ends when the paint's other effects end." special="A war painter can infuse only a skald spell whose level is at least 2 levels lower than the highest-level skald spell he can cast. He can infuse any targeted spell in a painted ally, even if its range is personal." incrementUse=He~7~6 replace="Lore master"}

warlord.name: Warlord
description: ## Warlord
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/49›

The warlord wields his force of personality like a weapon, intimidating his allies and ensuring that his followers heed his commands. The warlord doesn't inspire his followers so much as instill fear in them. Always watching his foes for moments of vulnerability, the warlord seeks to exploit his enemies' weaknesses to gain victory.
::ab[Intimidating Prowess]{icon=power ability="A warlord gains ‹feat/Intimidating Prowess› as a bonus feat." replace="Scribe Scroll feat"}
::ab[Unshakable (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A warlord develops an unshakable resolve, gaining a +2 bonus on saving throws against fear effects." replace=Well-versed}
::ab[Raging Song (Su)]{head icon=power info="A warlord gains the following *raging song.*"}

::ab[Fear Me (Su)]{next l=5 icon=protect ability="The warlord can remind his allies that failing him is far more frightening than the opposition. This *raging song* works like ‹performance/distraction›, except that it applies to saving throws against fear effects instead of patterns and figments, and the warlord attempts an Intimidate check with a -10 penalty instead of a Perform check. Additionally, the warlord's allies can use the warlord's Intimidate check result (with the -10 penalty) as the DC for attempts to demoralize them using Intimidate." replace="Spell kenning"}

::ab[Distraction]{next flavor="Text from the *distraction* ability" icon=def-protect ability="A bard can use his performance to counter magic effects that depend on sight. Each round of the *distraction,* he makes a Perform (act, comedy, dance, or oratory) skill check. Any creature within 30 feet of the bard (including the bard himself) that is affected by an illusion (pattern) or illusion (figment) magical attack may use the bard's Perform check result in place of its saving throw if, after the saving throw is rolled, the Perform skill check proves to be higher. If a creature within range of the *distraction* is already under the effect of a non-instantaneous illusion (pattern) or illusion (figment) magical attack, it gains another saving throw against the effect each round it sees the *distraction,* but it must use the bard's Perform skill check result for the save. *Distraction* does not work on effects that don't allow saves. *Distraction* relies on visual components."}
::ab[Minions (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power ability="A warlord attracts a group of followers through his fearsome reputation and gains the ‹feat/Leadership› feat as a bonus feat." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Intimidated Push (Ex)]{l=8 icon=protect ability="A warlord inspires fear in his cohort and followers, ensuring they do not fail and thus suffer his wrath. When he uses *inspired rage,* his cohort increases her morale bonus on Will saves from *inspired rage* by 1 and his followers increase their morale bonuses on Will saves from *inspired rage* by 2." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

wyrm_singer.name: Wyrm Singer
description: ## Wyrm Singer
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/14›
Wyrm singers spin fragments of the story of the ongoing struggle between noble Apsu and wicked Dahak.
::ab[Wyrm Song (Su)]{head icon=power info="A wyrm singer gains the following *raging songs.*"}

::ab[Draconic Rage (Su)]{next l=1 icon=boost-def ability="A wyrm singer can kindle an echo of ancient rage felt between warring dragon clans in his allies. This ability acts as *inspired rage,* except instead of *inspire rage's* normal bonuses, those affected gain a morale bonus on melee attack and damage rolls equal to 2 + ‹misc/one-eighth› of his skald level. Those affected also gain a morale bonus on saving throws against paralysis and sleep effects equal to 2 + one-fourth of his skald level. They still take a -1 penalty to their AC." replace="Inspired rage"}

::ab[Wyrm Saga (Su)]{next l=14 icon=magic ability="A wyrm singer embraces the essence of the draconic histories, allowing his allies to manifest aspects of a dragon in their physical forms. The wyrm singer selects a single ally within 60 feet to take on a draconic aspect (as per ‹spell/form of the dragon I›) of a type of the wyrm singer's choice. The ally cannot use the breath weapon attack provided by form of the dragon. The wyrm singer must expend 1 round of *raging song* each round to maintain wyrm saga, and can affect only a single ally at a time." replace="Song of the fallen"}

::ab[Breath Weapon (Su)]{l=12 icon=cone-line swift="Once per day, a wyrm singer can grant a breath weapon attack to himself or an ally affected by his draconic rage *raging song.* Using the breath weapon is a @HLstandard_action, and it affects creatures in a 30-foot cone or a 60-foot line. The breath weapons deals 1d6 points of damage per 2 skald levels the wyrm singer has, and is of an energy type of the wyrm singer's choice (acid, cold, electricity, or fire). Creatures caught in the area can attempt a Reflex save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the wyrm singer's skald level + his Charisma modifier) to halve the damage." replace="Rage power gained at 12th level"}
